paper.title_en: Antikick Relation in High-Energy Head-On Collisions of Spinning Black Holes
paper.abstract_en: The collision of black holes at relativistic speeds probes gravity in its most extreme dynamical regime. While the maximum gravitational recoil from \emph{grazing} high-energy collisions ($\approx28\,562$~km/s, i.e., $\sim0.1c$) and the maximum radiated energy $E_{\rm rad}$ and remnant spin $α_f^{\max}$ from such encounters ($E_{\rm rad}/M_{\rm ADM}\approx32\%$ where $M_{\rm ADM}$ is the ADM mass, and $α_f^{\max}\approx0.987$) have been established previously~\cite{Healy:2022jbh,Healy:2024lhl}, here we focus on the \emph{head-on} high-energy collision of equal-mass spinning black holes and on the detailed structure of the resulting recoil. Performing a sequence of full numerical simulations for spin magnitudes $s=0.5,0.65$, and $0.8$ over a range of initial momenta $γv$, we characterize the peak recoil $V_p$, the final recoil $V_f$, and the antikick $ΔV\equiv V_f-V_p$, and we provide phenomenological fits of their dependence on $γv$ and $s$. We complement these results with a zero-frequency-limit (ZFL) analysis of the radiated energy and momentum, a quasinormal-mode model of the antikick, and a superposed boosted double-Kerr close-limit estimate. We find that in the relativistic regime ($γv>1$) the peak and final recoil are directly proportional, $V_p\approx7.4\,V_f$ (equivalently $ΔV \approx-6.4\,V_f$), largely independent of both the initial momentum and the spin magnitude, pointing to a common post-merger relaxation. While the ZFL predicts a leading linear-in-spin dependence, the close-limit analysis predicts a leading $s^3$ dependence of the recoil amplitude; with the three spin magnitudes studied here the empirical exponent is $s^{1.27\pm0.08}$, motivating an even higher energy collision spin sequence study.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 论文针对自旋黑洞对心高能碰撞的反冲进行了详细数值模拟和理论分析，与广义相对论基础高度相关，方法严谨，结果具有新颖性。
decision.title_zh: 高能自旋黑洞对心碰撞中的反冲关系
decision.abstract_zh: 黑洞在相对论速度下的碰撞探测了引力在最极端动力学机制中的表现。虽然我们已经建立了从掠射高能碰撞中产生的最大引力反冲（约28,562 km/s，即约0.1c）以及最大辐射能量E_rad和残余自旋α_f^{max}（其中E_rad/M_ADM≈32%，M_ADM为ADM质量，α_f^{max}≈0.987），但本文聚焦于等质量自旋黑洞的高能对心碰撞及其产生的反冲的详细结构。通过对自旋大小s=0.5、0.65和0.8以及初始动量γv范围内的一系列完整数值模拟，我们表征了峰值反冲V_p、最终反冲V_f和反冲ΔV≡V_f-V_p，并提供了它们对γv和s依赖性的唯象拟合。我们利用零频极限（ZFL）分析辐射的能量和动量、准正则模式模型描述反冲以及叠加的加速双Kerr近距离极限估计来补充这些结果。我们发现，在相对论区域（γv>1），峰值反冲和最终反冲成正比，V_p≈7.4 V_f（等价地，ΔV≈-6.4 V_f），这很大程度上与初始动量和自旋大小无关，表明存在共同的合并后弛豫过程。虽然ZFL预测了领先的线性自旋依赖性，但近距离极限分析预测了反冲振幅的领先s^3依赖性；在研究的三个自旋大小中，经验指数为s^{1.27±0.08}，这促使我们进行更高能量碰撞的自旋序列研究。
